presentation: contract-accurate playback of the current Full YOLO state machine, anchored to a completed canonical Obsidian batch

actors.authority: canonical verification, leases, routing, checkpoints, and sequential Obsidian mutations
one exact leased ticket and pinned file scope; no Obsidian routing or commit

steps: derive the smallest graph-ready batch
verify registry-compatible implementer and reviewer
disclose exact scope, workspace, destinations, stop conditions, and optional blocked-remediation limit
obtain explicit user authorization
create one reusable batch handoff and reciprocal links without starting another batch
verify all five session capabilities and complete canonical batch authority
map the exact Wayfinder capsule and recheck freshness
acquire one atomic batch lease and owner heartbeat
persist the exact runtime checkpoint with state active
verify current ticket, dependency frontier, assignments, fixed point, rollback, and safe workspace
run red-first tracer-bullet tests
make the minimum root-cause change inside pinned scope
run focused, full, static, privacy, and bounded diff checks
record durable release evidence without committing
set ticket in-review and preserve implementation evidence
finalize same-note state and advance Wayfinder generation once
move the same batch handoff from implement to review
repair exact reciprocal links sequentially
verify source absence, destination integrity, hashes, links, plan, hub, and zero-error Markdown
acquire a fresh review lease and persist an active checkpoint
pin the exact fixed point and bounded review target
run fresh read-only Standards and Specification axes separately
Soher Coordinator reruns required checks and verifies implementation remained unchanged
record both dispositions durably
increment only the current ticket's normal remediation cycle
return the same handoff to implementation and repair links
after the second changes-required result require root-cause data flow, violated invariant, representation matrix, and adversarial tests
after the fourth changes-required result stop at automation-blocked
run a separately leased diagnosis-only phase without implementation
prove one recoverable technical defect inside unchanged ticket scope
increment the ticket-owned blocked attempt exactly once and route
later implementation reproduces the blocker red before the minimum fix
later fresh review either resolves the attempt or returns to ready/exhausted without incrementing it
an exhausted ticket stays paused until the user explicitly adds 1–3 attempts for the unchanged target and scope; repeated grants preserve history and the cumulative limit may not exceed 12
complete only after both review axes and all Soher Coordinator checks pass
preserve the completed ticket's remediation history
if a batch member remains, bind the successor and reset its ticket-owned counters
move the same handoff back to implement and let a later dispatcher trigger start it
verify every member completed and the exact batch exit gate passed
set the handoff review-complete with inactive review authority
move only the same handoff to its pre-authorized initiative-scoped completed destination
repair exact links sequentially and run the post-archive consistency gate
expose the next graph frontier without creating, authorizing, or starting the next batch

observedCompletedPath: T03 normal remediation cycle 3 implementation completed
fresh Standards and Spec review returned CHANGES_REQUIRED for the fourth time
workflow stopped at automation-blocked with post-block authority ready
separate diagnosis-and-route consumed blocked-remediation attempt 2 of 2
later implementation reproduced two blockers red before the minimum correction
later fresh Standards and Spec reviews both returned PASS
Soher Coordinator verification passed without implementation mutation
T03 completed and blocked-remediation state became resolved
V1-S4 exit gate passed and the same handoff moved to its pinned Completed destination
hardStops: missing capability, malformed authority, stale capsule, or lost lease
changed workspace, scope, security boundary, dependency graph, or fixed point
required check failure, ambiguous defect, or inseparable unrelated change
commit, push, publication, deployment, destruction, network entitlement, or next-batch action
exhausted or malformed remediation authority, a selector grant above 3 attempts, or a cumulative blocked-remediation limit above 12
